Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (concealment of identity)
Identification.ShortTitle: Preventing Persons from Concealing Their Identity during Riots and Unlawful Assemblies Act

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Criminal Code to make it an offence to wear a mask or other disguise to conceal one’s identity while taking part in a riot or an unlawful assembly.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Preventing Persons from Concealing Their Identity during Riots and Unlawful Assemblies Act.
Section 65 of the Criminal Code is re-numbered as subsection 65(1) and amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person who commits an offence under subsection (1) while wearing a mask or other disguise to conceal their identity without lawful excuse is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years.

Section.Text: Section 66 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 66(1) and amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person who commits an offence under subsection (1) while wearing a mask or other disguise to conceal their identity without lawful excuse is guilty of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding five years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction.
